title: Glass Anatomy finds stillness on Severance Songs
subtitle: 7.4 — The Rotterdam trio trades maximalism for negative space and lands its sharpest record yet
metaDescription: Review of Severance Songs by Glass Anatomy.
heroImageAlt: Band silhouettes on a stage under crossed spotlights
bodyHtml: Glass Anatomy have spent eight years building toward noise and now, on their fourth record, they have decided to stop. Severance Songs is an album of held breath.

title: Lena Moreau returns with Afterimage, her most personal record
subtitle: 8.1 — Fractured piano and close-mic vocals anchor an album that earns every quiet moment
metaDescription: Review of Afterimage by Lena Moreau.
heroImageAlt: Single lit window in a glass skyscraper reflecting sunset clouds
bodyHtml: Lena Moreau wrote Afterimage over three years and it sounds like it. The album moves in circles, returning to the same melodic fragments with fresh context each time.

title: Cassidy Null live at the Hague: a set that only works in person
subtitle: The Berlin producer built a two-hour structure around one degrading kick-drum sample
metaDescription: Live review of Cassidy Null at Rewire Festival, the Hague.
heroImageAlt: Lightning refracting through tall glass panels above still water
bodyHtml: You could feel the bass in the ceiling before you heard it. Cassidy Null opened with forty seconds of silence and then the kick arrived, already degraded, already tired.

title: The Pale Meridian: Hollow Hours EP review
subtitle: 6.8 — Promising architecture, cautious execution
metaDescription: EP review of Hollow Hours by The Pale Meridian.
heroImageAlt: Open backpack revealing a glowing Gothic cathedral inside
bodyHtml: Hollow Hours is the kind of debut EP that shows you exactly where a band is going and makes you wish they were already there. Four tracks, each one reaching for something just outside its grasp.

title: Reissue round-up: three records from the analogue revival era
subtitle: Ostinato, RVNG, and Light in the Attic resurface overlooked sessions from 1979 to 1988
metaDescription: Reissue reviews from Ostinato, RVNG, and Light in the Attic.
bodyHtml: Reissue culture has a short memory problem. These three labels are quietly working against that, digging for sessions that never found their audience the first time.

title: Interview: Arvo Kask on building instruments from bridge cables
subtitle: The Estonian composer on field recording, tension, and why concert halls are the wrong room
metaDescription: Interview with Arvo Kask, composer and instrument builder.
bodyHtml: Arvo Kask builds his instruments from what the city discards. His most recent project used steel cable salvaged from a demolished footbridge in Tallinn.

title: Festival preview: what to catch at Unsound Krakow 2026
subtitle: Twenty acts worth the trip, from headliner Pharmakon to the quietly essential side rooms
metaDescription: Preview guide for Unsound Festival Krakow 2026.
bodyHtml: Unsound has spent two decades earning the right to program whatever it wants. This year's lineup reads less like a festival bill and more like a research agenda.

title: Column: the genre tag is a service agreement
subtitle: Streaming platforms built a world where names do the work that listening used to do
metaDescription: Opinion column on genre classification and streaming platforms.
bodyHtml: When a label files an album under post-ambient-folk, they are not describing the music. They are describing the audience they hope the platform will route it toward.
